records.ety: [F. lection, L. electio, fr. eligere to choose out. See Elect, a.]

defs: The act of choosing; choice; selection.
The act of choosing a person to fill an office, or to membership in a society, as by ballot, uplifted hands, or viva voce; as, the election of a president or a mayor.
quotes: Corruption in elections is the great enemy of freedom.

defs: Power of choosing; free will; liberty to choose or act.

quotes: To use men with much difference and election is good.

defs: Divine choice; predestination of individuals as objects of mercy and salvation; -- one of the “five points” of Calvinism.
quotes: There is a remnant according to the election of grace.

defs: The choice, made by a party, of two alternatives, by taking one of which, the chooser is excluded from the other.

quotes: He has made his election to walk, in the main, in the old paths.
